title: tulipa praecox subsp. foxiana
intro: Tulipa praecox subsp. foxiana, also known as Tulipa 'Fox', is a species of tulip that belongs to the Liliaceae family. It is known for its unique and vibrant flowers, which are often used in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Tulipa praecox subsp. foxiana include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as having potential effects on the cardiovascular and nervous systems. Herbal preparations of Tulipa praecox subsp. foxiana are typically made from the dried bulb, which can be used to create teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
